sources_exist.note: Six citation URLs resolve. The exact inputs.pricing_structure.url path did not yield an accessible page; search located a different Gould+Partners billing-utilization landing-page path, which cannot substitute for auditing the exact citation.

figures_match_sources.note: None of the seven citations supports its full score-affecting claim: the sources do not establish six active consolidators, 5.0x/9.0x normalized PR multiples at the required scales, 25% material adoption, 5-8-year workflow analogs, 25% age-60+ ownership, or the asserted 70/20/10 pricing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role shares sum to 1.0 and contributions sum to 0.4468, but the 32% back-office/admin bucket materially exceeds the prompt's 20-25% guidance and, at a 0.60 automatable fraction, contributes 0.192—43% of the total AI-replaceable share—without occupational evidence. t50_years=3.0 and pi_dist=0.28 also depend on unsupported analog and billing-mix evidence.

rubric_consistent.note: No Stage-4 flags were assigned. The record nevertheless violates the source and judgment requirements of the frozen rubric. C=0.784 is mechanically below the thresholds.json 1.0 kill floor, but that correct mechanical consequence does not cure the unsupported capture inputs used to produce it.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and the PRESSURED terminal classification is directionally plausible. However, no_proxy_confirmed treats unsupported social-media/media-monitoring diffusion claims as evidence for A, and pricing_model_deflation presents an unaudited 70% hour-scoped retainer share and 42% utilization statistic as established facts.

source_audits.note: The CommPRO article reports 79 communications/PR deals in 2024, 35% involving PE, and describes TPG-owned Sodali's acquisition strategy. It does not enumerate six PE-backed PR platforms with add-ons in the 24 months through 2026-07-20, and several platforms named in the record are not evidenced on the page.

The April 2026 valuation blog says healthy independent PR firms with $1-3M EBITDA have a median around 5.5x and gives a general 4-7x range. It does not support the record's 5.0x median, does not disclose the underlying PR transaction sample, and does not document the claimed owner-comp conversion to 5.0x.

The USC page discusses generational attitudes, including that two-thirds of Gen Z PR professionals expect positive AI impact and one-fourth expect fewer entry-level roles. It reports neither two-thirds frequent AI use nor 18% measurable workflow integration and does not establish value=0.25 for PR firms.

The blog says strategic buyers may pay 8-9x above $3M EBITDA and describes 7-9x specialty ranges, but it does not report a 9.0x transaction/comps benchmark for $10M+ EBITDA PR platforms or substantiate the record's advertising-relative adjustment.
The page is a 2024 PR M&A review. It contains no adoption curve for social-media management or media-monitoring SaaS, no 2010-2016 diffusion claim, and no measured 5-8-year time to 50%.

The November 2025 Census release supplies no owner-age statistics. It does not support 37% Baby Boomer ownership, the 13.0%-to-16.3% age-65+ self-employment change, or the interpolated 25% age-60+ value for PR agencies.

The exact cited URL did not return an accessible page. A different Gould+Partners landing page confirms that a proprietary billing/utilization report exists, but neither the exact citation nor accessible replacement content establishes 70% retainer use, a 70/20/10 revenue mix, the example hour calculation, or 62.7% labor cost.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators (https://www.commpro.biz/news/ma-in-pr-is-heating-up-as-buyers-line-up-for-2025-acquisitions), aggregate 2024 deal counts and one named strategy do not support value=6 active PE-backed add-on platforms in the last 24 months. Re-run with an enumerated six-platform list and dated PR-agency add-ons for every counted platform.

At inputs.buy_mult (https://exitvalue.ai/blog/how-to-value-pr-public-relations-firm), the source reports a 5.5x median for healthy $1-3M EBITDA independents, not 5.0x, and does not provide the record's normalization chain. Re-run with actual small-firm transaction evidence and document the conversion from any SDE or founder-influenced earnings to post-market-owner-comp EBITDA.

At inputs.exit_mult (https://exitvalue.ai/blog/how-to-value-pr-public-relations-firm), a statement that strategic multiples can stretch to 8-9x above $3M does not establish 9.0x for $10M+ EBITDA platform exits. Re-run with recent disclosed $10M+ transactions or credible comparable-company evidence at the required scale.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct (https://annenberg.usc.edu/news/research-and-impact/center-public-relations-global-communication-report-uncovers-key-industry), the cited USC page contains attitudes rather than use rates and supports neither the quoted 18%/two-thirds anchors nor value=0.25. Re-run with firm-level PR adoption evidence defining material core-workflow use.

At inputs.historical_analogs (https://www.provokemedia.com/latest/article/2024-m-a-review-big-headlines-and-lots-of-smaller-deals), an M&A review is unrelated to the claimed social-media and media-monitoring adoption timelines. Re-run with measured penetration data for comparable PR workflow technologies before deriving inputs.t50_years.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct (https://www.census.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2025/business-owner-characteristics.html), the cited release contains no age data and cannot support value=0.25. Re-run with an age-compatible owner dataset and show the industry adjustment or interpolation.

At inputs.pricing_structure and inputs.pi_dist (https://www.gould-partners.com/billing-rates-utilization-report/), the exact URL is inaccessible and no audited content supports the 70/20/10 mix, hour-scoped retainer claim or utilization statistics. Re-run with an accessible report page or primary survey and derive pi_dist from measured mechanism shares.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[5], the 32% back-office/admin labor share exceeds the prompt's 20-25% industry guidance without evidence and drives 0.192 of the 0.4468 total. Re-run with a sourced, mutually exclusive function allocation; identify the occupations included in the residual and avoid classifying account/client delivery staff as highly automatable administration.

At cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation, the record states unsupported diffusion timelines, a 70% hour-scoped retainer share and a 42% utilization figure as facts. Re-run both cross-checks after replacing the source inputs and distinguish temporary recovery of over-service from evidence of client fee deflation at renewal.
